intro: Uragoga rufipes, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Uragoga has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its reported analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Uragoga is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and combat infections.
